title: Pipeline Architecture
use_for: designing architecture around explicit processing stages and data flow
treating optimization as a consequence of pipeline structure rather than late cleanup
defining predictable recompute boundaries and execution paths
do_not_use_for: generic OOP layering without dataflow concerns
rules: organize systems as explicit stages over explicit data
make stage inputs and outputs reviewable
optimize by limiting recompute scope rather than by hiding work
prefer stable stage order and deterministic data ownership
procedure: Identify the data products the system must produce.
Split the work into explicit stages with clear inputs and outputs.
Define ownership of intermediate state and derived products.
Define recompute and invalidation boundaries per stage.
Choose an execution model only after stage boundaries are clear.

primary_when: the request is about architecture built around data-oriented processing stages, recompute boundaries, or pipeline-first optimization
the request treats architecture and runtime optimization as one pipeline design problem
must_not_own_when: the request is mainly about one isolated algorithm, one container type, or generic startup wiring

solves: how to make architecture itself carry the optimization model
how to replace broad opaque systems with explicit stage-based processing

core_principles: Optimization should fall out of explicit pipeline structure.
Stages should produce named data products rather than hidden side effects.
Recompute scope should be a first-class architectural concept.
constraints: Each stage must have a reviewable input and output boundary.
Derived data ownership must remain explicit.
The pipeline must not depend on hidden cross-stage mutation.
decision_rules: Use a pipeline-first design when the system repeatedly transforms source data into derived products.
Split stages where recompute cost, ownership, or invalidation boundaries differ meaningfully.
Reject architectures that cannot explain which stage must rerun after a local state change.

preferred_patterns: named stages with explicit inputs and outputs
derived data products retained between runs
recompute boundaries aligned to owned stage outputs
avoid_patterns: hidden shared mutable state across stages
architecture that only becomes data-oriented during optimization patches
broad monolithic update passes with unclear products

recipes.name: Turn a broad update loop into a stage pipeline
recipes.when: Use when one large update path mixes many transformations and becomes hard to optimize.
steps: List the derived outputs the loop really produces.
Group operations into explicit stages by data dependency.
Assign owned intermediate products to each stage.
Define when each stage must rerun and when it can stay clean.

pitfalls: Splitting stages without clear data products.
Keeping hidden cross-stage mutation that defeats recompute boundaries.
Treating the pipeline as documentation instead of runtime architecture.
review_checklist: Are outputs and intermediate products named?
Can recompute scope be explained per stage?
Is optimization embodied in architecture rather than patched in later?

references.title: Local Reference: Pipeline Architecture
references.interpretation: Use this first for stage-first architecture, ownership of derived data, and pipeline-as-optimization reasoning.
references.why: Use as the primary local guide for the domain's architectural stance.

references.title: Local Reference: Execution Model
references.interpretation: Use this after stage boundaries are defined to choose sequencing and dispatch strategy.
references.why: Use to connect architecture to runtime execution.
